system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep    yes;

maxCo             0.5;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    3;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        U               0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='epsilon', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

dimensions      [0 2 -3 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 10;

boundaryField
{

    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 10;
    }

    {

    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 10;
    }

    lowerWall
    {

        value           uniform 10;
    }

    }
}

}

transportModel  Newtonian;

kinematicViscosityScheme constant;

nu              1e-04;')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
problem while reading header for object nut

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/pitzDaily/3/0/nut at line 1.

    From function virtual Foam::autoPtr<Foam::ISstream> Foam::fileOperations::uncollatedFileOperation::readStream(Foam::regIOobject&, const Foam::fileName&, const Foam::word&, bool) const
    in file global/fileOperations/uncollatedFileOperation/uncollatedFileOperation.C at line 560.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
problem while reading header for object nut

file: .../runs/pitzDaily/3/0/nut at line 1.
```

This means the solver is actively trying to read a `nut` field file from `0/nut`, and it's finding garbage/corrupted data at line 1. The file exists but contains invalid content.

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the history carefully, the `0/nut` file has persisted through all 5 attempts despite recommendations to delete it. Each attempt showed the same error, suggesting the file keeps getting restored/recreated. The current state shows the file exists with some malformed content at line 1.

The fundamental problem is that **OpenFOAM 10's `kEpsilon` model does not require or use a user-provided `nut` field file**. The `nut` field is computed internally from `k` and `epsilon`. Having a `nut` file with boundary conditions causes problems because:

1. The file likely got corrupted over successive failed runs
2. Even if clean, the `nutWallFunction` boundary type isn't recognized for this context in OpenFOAM 10
3. The solver tries to parse it and fails at the header level

## New Approach (Different from Previous Attempts)

Instead of continuing to suggest "delete the nut file" (which clearly hasn't worked), I'll address the root cause by ensuring the `kEpsilon` model works properly with the existing setup.

### 1. Replace the `0/nut` file with a minimal placeholder

Create a minimal `0/nut` file that simply declares the field without boundary conditions, or better yet, use the `calculated` type consistently. The file should look like:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      nut;
}

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
    }
    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            calculated;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            calculated;
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

The key point is using `type calculated;` at all boundaries. This way `nut` is computed from `k` and `epsilon` at runtime, which is exactly how the `kEpsilon` model works.

### 2. Alternatively, restructure the `physicalProperties` file

Some versions of OpenFOAM 10 expect the kinematic viscosity to be specified as a `dimensionedScalar` rather than a bare `nu`. Try changing `constant/physicalProperties` to:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

transportModel  Newtonian;

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

kinematicViscosityScheme constant;

nu              [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0] 1e-04;
```

Adding explicit dimensions `[0 2 -1 0 0 0 0]` to `nu` may help the solver properly resolve the viscosity units when computing `nut`.

### 3. Ensure `k` and `epsilon` have consistent wall functions

The `k` file uses `kqRWallFunction` at walls, which is correct. The `epsilon` file uses `epsilonWallFunction`, which is also correct. Keep these as-is.

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix `0/nut`**: Replace the current corrupted file with one using `type calculated;` on all boundaries, or remove it entirely if possible.
2. **Enhance `physicalProperties`**: Add explicit dimensions to the `nu` entry to ensure proper unit handling.
3. **Keep all other files unchanged** — they appear correct.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': '0/nut', 'changes': 'Replace entire file with minimal valid nut field using type calculated for all boundaries; set internalField uniform 0; set dimensions to [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0]; set inlet type calculated; set outlet type calculated; set upperWall type calculated; set lowerWall type calculated; set frontAndBack type empty'}, {'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Add explicit dimensions keyword before nu entry: dimensions [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0]; then nu [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0] 1e-04 to ensure proper unit resolution'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
